behavior_summary: Implements the short-examine (U8P_GUARD8::look) and full-interaction (use), area-trigger (enterFastArea), and periodic maintenance (schedule) behaviors for a guard NPC (GUARD8).
GUARD8::look: when a player 'looks' at the guard, the script checks if the NPC is dead and issues a short bark describing the guard as either 'guardsman ' or 'dead guardsman '.

U8P_GUARD8::enterFastArea: when an actor enters the guard's trigger region, if the guard is dead it is revived (clrDead, clrInCombat) and teleported to a fixed location; if alive, the script spawns reaction methods to produce guard behavior.
U8P_GUARD8::schedule: periodic heartbeat that spawns a check METHOD and, if that method indicates a particular result, clears the dead flag and spawns a reset/init METHOD.

U8P_GUARD8::use: full-talk flow that barks, spawns longer dialog/behavior methods, examines several persistent world flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree) to possibly issue an urgent broadcast, uses guard-specific flags (guard8Met, guard8Pissed, guard8Thanked) to select greeting branches, offers a short choice menu via Item::ask and responds accordingly; it updates persistent flags (sets guard8Met, may clear guard8Pissed, may set guard8Thanked).
notable_control_flow: GUARD8::look branches immediately on Npc::isDead to choose a one-line bark, then frees temporaries and suspends.

enterFastArea has an early revival path (Npc::clrDead, Npc::clrInCombat, Npc::teleport) vs an alive path that spawns two asynchronous METHODS for reaction; those spawns are used rather than inline behavior.
schedule spawns a METHOD::143A with several args and inspects the spawn result; on a specific result it revives the NPC and spawns an initialization METHOD::133F.

use uses a small integer accumulator (local BP-06h) incremented by reads of hydrosFree/hasHeart/pyrosFree to select an urgent-message branch; otherwise it does multi-branch dialog driven by guard8Met/guard8Pissed, random choice (urandom), and player's selection from Item::ask.
open_questions: Exact textual content and localization of the longer dialog blocks used in use() beyond the two short barks (only shorthand descriptions are present).
Precise semantics and return protocol of the spawned METHODs (IDs 091C, 15E7, 143A, 133F, 0BCF, 1090) — what each METHOD does and what return codes they produce.
Whether guard8Thanked is set in this file or external code paths (scout facts mention it but snippet context is partial).
Exact coordinates/map identifiers used by Npc::teleport (map/tile/z/orientation values are present but their in-game meaning needs mapping to world coordinates).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD8::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player/actor 'U8P_GUARD8::look' (short examine) by barking a one-line identification that differs if the guard is dead or alive.
entry_conditions: Event 0: player/process inspects or 'looks at' the guard (short interaction).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::look
function_map.role: Short-examine handler that chooses an alive vs dead bark.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger-region entry handler; revives/teleports dead guard or spawns reaction methods when alive.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler/heartbeat that spawns a check METHOD and may revive/reset the guard based on the result.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD8::use
function_map.role: Full 'use' / talk interaction implementing multi-branch dialog, flag updates, and choice handling.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Query NPC death state used by U8P_GUARD8::look and enterFastArea.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit spoken/text lines to the player (used for barks and long dialog).

function_map.name: Item::ask
function_map.role: Present a selectable list of dialog options and return the chosen string.

internal_calls.purpose: Start an asynchronous reaction/behavior when the guard is alive and the player enters the trigger.
Start a second reaction/behavior for area entry (complements METHOD::091C).
Spawn a scheduled check METHOD and inspect its result to decide whether to revive/reset the NPC.
Spawn reset/initialization behavior after clearing dead state based on schedule check result.
Kick off a longer dialog/behavior thread at start of the talk interaction.
Spawn an urgent broadcast/behavior when the urgent-message counter indicates multiple faction frees.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether to describe the guard as dead or alive.
Emit the one-line identification to the player (either 'guardsman ' or 'dead guardsman ').
Clear the NPC dead flag to revive it when a relevant actor enters the area.
Clear in-combat state when reviving/resetting the NPC.
Move the revived NPC to a predefined location/orientation.
Pick between alternate short replies to vary dialog.
Show dialog choices (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, Bye) and receive player's selection string.
Call global utility routines as part of conversation setup/cleanup (UI or script helpers).

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Influences the urgent-message counter in use(); presence increases likelihood of the urgent broadcast branch.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Picked to select one of two long-warning dialog blocks and increments urgent counter; affects which long bark is used.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Similar to hydrosFree: increments urgent-message counter used in use().

reads.name: guard8Met
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether to use a 'first meeting' greeting branch or the 'met before' greeting.

reads.name: guard8Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Alters greeting into an angry short reply vs normal greeting flow.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Selects which authority the guard cites in the 'duty' response (Devon vs alternative).

reads.name: guard8Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Used to avoid repeating a unique 'thank you' dialog (presence suppresses thank-you branch).

writes.name: guard8Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 on the player's first full talk so future interactions take the 'met' branch.

writes.name: guard8Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared (written 0) along some dialog paths to calm the guard after resolving anger.

writes.name: guard8Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set when the guard thanks the player to avoid repeating that line in future conversations.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the short-examine (U8P_GUARD8::look) and full-interaction (use), area-trigger (enterFastArea), and periodic maintenance (schedule) behaviors for a guard NPC (GUARD8).
